items.prompt: A kid with no armor walked up to a giant who'd been mocking everyone. Who was he?
items.verse: 1 Samuel 17:45 — "You come against me with sword and spear, but I come against you in the name of the Lord Almighty."
items.takeaway: David didn't win because he was bigger. He stepped up because God was with him—and that changes everything when you're scared.
items.answer: David

items.prompt: Fill in the blank: "Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid… the Lord your God will be with you ___ you go."
items.verse: Joshua 1:9
items.takeaway: God said this right before Joshua had to lead people into hard, unknown territory. Courage isn't pretending you're not afraid—it's moving forward anyway with God.
items.answer: wherever

items.prompt: Finish this power line: "I can do all things through ___ who strengthens me."
items.verse: Philippians 4:13
items.takeaway: This isn't a sports hype quote—Paul wrote it while he was stuck in prison. Real strength comes from Christ, not from pretending life is easy.
items.answer: Christ

items.prompt: Jesus' friends thought they were going to die in a storm. What did Jesus do?
items.verse: Mark 4:39 — He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, "Quiet! Be still!" Then the wind died down and it was completely calm.
items.takeaway: The storm was real. Their fear was real. Jesus wasn't panicking—and he's still in control when life feels chaotic.
items.answer: He calmed the storm

items.prompt: Who got thrown into a lions' den for praying—and walked out alive the next morning?
items.verse: Daniel 6:22 — "My God sent his angel, and he shut the mouths of the lions. They have not hurt me."
items.takeaway: Daniel didn't cheat or hide his faith to fit in. Standing for what's right can cost you—but God is bigger than the lions.
items.answer: Daniel

items.prompt: True or false: "You're too young to make a difference for God."
items.verse: 1 Timothy 4:12 — "Don't let anyone look down on you because you are young, but set an example… in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith."
items.takeaway: Age isn't a disqualifier. How you live—honestly, bravely, kindly—can influence people older than you.
items.answer: False — you can set an example now

items.prompt: If God is for you, who can actually win against you?
items.verse: Romans 8:31 — "If God is for us, who can be against us?"
items.takeaway: Not "life will be easy." It means the final score isn't decided by bullies, bad grades, or bad days. God doesn't abandon his team.
items.answer: No one ultimately — God is for you

items.prompt: You're confused about a big decision. What does Proverbs say to do instead of leaning only on your own gut?
items.verse: Proverbs 3:5-6 — "Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight."
items.takeaway: Trust + action. Ask God, listen, make a wise move. You don't have to figure out your whole future in one stressed-out night.
items.answer: Trust God and submit your ways to him

items.prompt: When you feel like you're not enough, what does Ephesians 2:10 call you?
items.verse: Ephesians 2:10 — "We are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do."
items.takeaway: Handiwork = masterpiece, not accident. You weren't a mistake, and your life has purpose—even on boring Tuesdays.
items.answer: God's handiwork (masterpiece)

items.prompt: Even walking through the "valley of the shadow of death," what does the psalmist say he won't fear?
items.verse: Psalm 23:4 — "Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me."
items.takeaway: It doesn't say you'll skip hard times. It says you're not walking through them alone.
items.answer: Evil / the dark valley — because God is with him

items.prompt: Sold by his own brothers, falsely accused, thrown in prison—years later he became a leader who saved nations. Who?
items.verse: Genesis 50:20 — "You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good."
items.takeaway: Your worst chapter isn't the whole book. God can turn betrayal and setbacks into something bigger than you can see right now.
items.answer: Joseph

items.prompt: Gideon felt weak and outnumbered. God told him to cut his army from 32,000 down to 300. Why?
items.verse: Judges 7:2 — "You have too many men. I cannot deliver Midian into their hands, or Israel would boast against me, 'My own strength has saved me.'"
items.takeaway: God often wins through the unlikely so nobody can say "I did this alone." Your weakness isn't always a disqualifier.
items.answer: So Israel would know God gave the victory, not their own strength

items.prompt: You're furious at someone. What does Ephesians 4:26 say about anger?
items.verse: Ephesians 4:26 — "In your anger do not sin: Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry."
items.takeaway: Anger happens. Sin doesn't have to. Deal with it instead of letting it turn into bitterness that eats you from the inside.
items.answer: Don't sin in your anger; don't stay angry forever

items.prompt: Your words can build someone up or wreck them. What does Proverbs 18:21 compare the tongue to?
items.verse: Proverbs 18:21 — "The tongue has the power of life and death."
items.takeaway: Roasts, gossip, and "just joking" comments hit harder than you think. You can be the kid who speaks life instead.
items.answer: Life and death — your words have real power

items.prompt: Finish it: "For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and a ___ mind."
items.verse: 2 Timothy 1:7
items.takeaway: Anxiety isn't your identity. Fear doesn't get the last word. God gives you what you need to think clearly and act with love.
items.answer: sound (self-disciplined)

items.prompt: Why did God send Jesus, according to John 3:16?
items.verse: John 3:16 — "For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life."
items.takeaway: The whole story starts with love—not with God waiting for you to mess up so he can punish you.
items.answer: Because he loved the world — so we could have eternal life through faith

items.prompt: Bad friends can drag you down. What does 1 Corinthians 15:33 warn?
items.verse: 1 Corinthians 15:33 — "Bad company corrupts good character."
items.takeaway: Who you hang with shapes who you become. Choose friends who pull you toward courage and honesty, not just trouble.
items.answer: Bad company corrupts good character

items.prompt: Jesus' last words in Matthew include "Go and make disciples." What promise comes with that mission?
items.verse: Matthew 28:20 — "Surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age."
items.takeaway: You're not sent out alone. Whatever you're called to do—speak up, help someone, lead by example—Jesus goes with you.
items.answer: I am with you always

items.prompt: You know what the right thing is but keep putting it off. James 4:17 calls that what?
items.verse: James 4:17 — "If anyone, then, knows the good they ought to do and doesn't do it, it is sin for them."
items.takeaway: It's not just about breaking rules—it's about ignoring the good you already know you should do. Action beats excuses.
items.answer: Sin — knowing good and not doing it

items.prompt: When you're running on empty, who does Isaiah say will "soar on wings like eagles"?
items.verse: Isaiah 40:31 — "Those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary."
items.takeaway: Burnout is real. Hoping in God isn't magic energy drink—it's trusting him enough to rest, wait, and keep going.
items.answer: Those who hope in the Lord

items.prompt: Three guys refused to bow to a golden statue and got thrown into a blazing furnace. What happened?
items.verse: Daniel 3:25 — The king looked in and said, "Look! I see four men walking around in the fire, unbound and unharmed… and the fourth looks like a son of the gods."
items.takeaway: They didn't compromise to avoid pain. God showed up in the fire—not always by removing it, but by being in it with them.
items.answer: They survived — God was with them in the fire

items.prompt: What's the "golden rule" Jesus gave in Matthew 7:12?
items.verse: Matthew 7:12 — "Do to others whatever you would like them to do to you."
items.takeaway: Simple test before you post, tease, or ignore someone: Would I want this done to me? That's the standard.
items.answer: Treat others how you want to be treated

items.prompt: Jeremiah thought he was too young when God called him. What did God say about his age?
items.verse: Jeremiah 1:7-8 — "Do not say, 'I am too young.' You must go to everyone I send you to and say whatever I command you. Do not be afraid of them, for I am with you."
items.takeaway: God doesn't wait until you're "old enough" on paper. He equips you for what he calls you to—and stays with you.
items.answer: Don't say you're too young — God is with you

items.prompt: Hebrews 12:1 says to throw off everything that slows you down and run with what?
items.verse: Hebrews 12:1-2 — "Let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus… who endured the cross… so we will not grow weary."
items.takeaway: Life is a long race, not one sprint. Drop the distractions and keep your eyes on Jesus when you want to quit.
items.answer: Perseverance — fixing your eyes on Jesus
